integrations_researched.name: xAI API (z.AI / Grok)
integrations_researched.description: xAI's API for accessing Grok models. The 'z.AI' mentioned in requirements is actually xAI (Elon Musk's AI company). The API is OpenAI-compatible.

verified_package.name: openai
verified_package.notes: xAI API is OpenAI-compatible - can use the openai package with custom baseURL

api_patterns.initialization: const client = new OpenAI({ apiKey: process.env.XAI_API_KEY, baseURL: 'https://api.x.ai/v1' })

gotchas: Vision models require specific image format (jpg/jpeg or png, max 20MiB)
For reasoning models, may need longer timeout (up to 3600s)
The 'coding plan API token' mentioned likely refers to a specific tier/plan of xAI API access

integrations_researched.name: OpenRouter
integrations_researched.description: OpenRouter provides a unified API to access 300+ AI models through a single endpoint. It's OpenAI-compatible and supports automatic fallbacks and cost-effective routing.

verified_package.name: openai
verified_package.notes: OpenRouter is OpenAI-compatible - can use the openai package with custom baseURL. There's also an official @openrouter/sdk package available.

alternative_package.name: @openrouter/sdk
alternative_package.notes: Official OpenRouter TypeScript SDK with additional features like callModel API

api_patterns.initialization: const client = new OpenAI({ baseURL: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1', apiKey: process.env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY, defaultHeaders: { 'HTTP-Referer': '<YOUR_SITE_URL>', 'X-Title': '<YOUR_APP_NAME>' } })

optional_headers.HTTP-Referer: Your site URL for rankings on openrouter.ai
optional_headers.X-Title: Your app name for rankings on openrouter.ai

gotchas: API key prefix is 'sk-or-' for OpenRouter keys
Response includes native_finish_reason alongside normalized finish_reason
Streaming responses may include usage statistics in final chunk
Some features depend on underlying model capabilities
HTTP-Referer and X-Title headers are optional but recommended for app attribution

features: Dual mode: client-side (SDK) and proxy mode (fetch)
Tool/function calling with streaming tool arguments
Message format conversion from ClippyJS to OpenAI format

key_config_options.apiKey: API key for direct client-side usage

implementation_recommendations.recommendation: Reuse OpenAI SDK approach for both providers
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Both xAI and OpenRouter APIs are OpenAI-compatible, meaning they use the same request/response format. The existing OpenAIProvider pattern can be adapted by simply changing baseURL and API key handling.
implementation_recommendations.approach: Create new provider classes that extend or clone the OpenAIProvider pattern with provider-specific configuration (baseURL, default models, etc.)

implementation_recommendations.recommendation: Create separate packages following existing convention
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Maintain consistency with existing @clippyjs/ai-openai and @clippyjs/ai-anthropic packages

implementation_recommendations.recommendation: Support both direct API and proxy modes
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Existing providers support both modes for flexibility in deployment
implementation_recommendations.approach: Maintain the same config pattern: apiKey for direct access, endpoint for proxy mode

implementation_recommendations.recommendation: For OpenRouter, consider exposing model routing features
implementation_recommendations.rationale: OpenRouter's key differentiator is its ability to route to different providers/models
implementation_recommendations.approach: Add optional configuration for model fallbacks and routing preferences

unverified_claims.reason: The requirements mention 'coding plan API token' but xAI documentation doesn't specifically reference this terminology. This may refer to a specific pricing tier, API access level, or team configuration on xAI's platform.
unverified_claims.mitigation: Implementation should use standard XAI_API_KEY environment variable. The 'coding plan' aspect is likely an account/billing configuration on xAI's end, not something affecting the code implementation.

key_finding.summary: Both xAI and OpenRouter are OpenAI-compatible APIs
key_finding.implication: The implementation is simpler than initially expected. Both providers can reuse the OpenAI SDK (already a dependency) with custom baseURL configuration. This means minimal new code is needed - essentially creating provider wrappers that configure the OpenAI client differently.
